svg.title: Federated Web Components Decision
svg.desc: Decision diagram showing how the design system, platform shell, and sub-applications relate, and why federated Web Components are the recommended default integration model.
svg.text: DESIGN SYSTEM, SHELL, AND SUB-APPLICATION RELATIONSHIPS

g.text: Navigation, headers, cards, tables, forms, status

svg.text: Consume same tokens and registry components
Plug into shell routes and navigation
Own domain workflows, data, and releases
✓ Shell owns nav, chrome, auth, routes, and loading
✓ Shell does not import remote Angular internals
✓ Direct runtime contract: load, register, mount
✓ Future remotes can use different frameworks
⚠ Shadow DOM ambiguity → Confirm DOM strategy
⚠ Styling inheritance gaps → Define token strategy
⚠ Context passing → Define attrs, props, events
Tokens, PrimeNG provider guidance, registry components, layout patterns, accessibility evidence, Storybook, and Zeroheight docs.
Remote manifests, element registration, token resolution, overlays, routing, lifecycle cleanup, accessibility, and performance tracking.
Proceed with federated Web Components as the default integration model.
